articles.title: What’s likely to move the market
articles.description: The S&P 500 snapped a nine-day winning streak on Wednesday as rising Treasury yields and oil prices kept stocks under pressure.

articles.title: Gas prices averaged $3.91 in Iowa the week of June 3
articles.description: The price of regular unleaded gasoline fell 21 cents from last week's price and is currently averaging $3.91 across Iowa, according to AAA and the Iowa Department of Agriculture and Land Stewardship's weekly fuel report.

articles.title: Canada’s Inflation Rate Just Jumped: 2 Stocks That Look Built for it
articles.description: Inflation is flaring again, and these two utility stocks let investors lean into essential electricity demand instead of chasing oil prices.

articles.title: US Fed says Iran war driving 'moderate-to-strong' inflation
articles.description: US inflation rose at a moderate to strong pace, driven largely by surging energy costs linked to the Iran war, the Federal Reserve said. Higher oil prices disrupted supply chains and lifted input costs, while consumer spending showed widening inequality, with lower-income households facing rising financial strain.

articles.title: Stock Market Today: Indexes Tumble, Bond Yields Surge Amid Fresh Iran Attacks
articles.description: The Dow fell over 500 points on Wednesday as traders assessed fresh strikes between the US and Iran that drove oil prices and bond yields higher.

articles.title: US stocks today: Dow Jones drops over 500 points as Middle East tensions escalate
articles.description: Wall Street closed lower as Middle East tensions and rising oil prices triggered inflation concerns and profit booking. Financials and tech stocks led declines, though chipmakers stayed resilient on AI optimism. Strong services data and steady labor conditions contrasted with geopolitical risks, while expectations for a potential Fed rate hike continued to climb.

articles.title: Wall Street Wobbles Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Rising Oil Prices
articles.description: Wall Street stocks declined as tensions in the Middle East and heightened crude prices spurred inflation concerns. The major U.S. stock indexes closed negatively, with financials and tech leading the drop. Investors' unease highlighted the ongoing conflict's impact on energy prices and the potential for systemic inflation.

articles.title: The MPC can afford to wait for more inflation and growth trends before it takes a call on raising rates
articles.description: The RBI is expected to keep interest rates unchanged despite rising oil prices and rupee weakness, as concerns over economic growth outweigh the case for tighter policy.
